from pyspark.sql import functions as F

SOURCE_FILE = f"{mpesc_base_path}/MPESC-SPREADSHEETS/MPESC-SPREADSHEETS-RAW/Case.csv"

load_file = (
    spark.read
        .format("csv")
        .option("header", True)
        .option("sep", ",")
        .option("multiLine", True)          
        .option("quote", '"')               # handle quoted fields
        .option("escape", '"')              # handle escaped quotes
        .option("mode", "PERMISSIVE")       # don’t fail on bad rows
        .option("inferSchema", False)       # safer for messy exports
        .load(SOURCE_FILE)
)

display(load_file.limit(10))
print("load_file rows:", load_file.count())

from pyspark.sql.functions import regexp_extract

df = load_file.select(
    regexp_extract("id", r"/(\d+)-", 1).alias("Case_ID"),
    "*"
    
)

display(df.limit(10))

# generate 
from pyspark.sql.functions import md5, regexp_replace

df_guid = df.select(
    regexp_replace(
        md5("id"),
        r"(.{8})(.{4})(.{4})(.{4})(.{12})",
        r"$1-$2-$3-$4-$5"
    ).alias("GUID")
    ,"*"
)

display(df_guid.limit(10))

out_addresses = f"{mpesc_base_path}/MPESC-SPREADSHEETS/MPESC-SPREADSHEETS-TRANSFORM/Cases-Transform"

(df_guid
    .coalesce(1)
    .write
    .mode("overwrite")
    .option("header", "true")
    # .option("sep", "|")          # explicit pipe delimiter
    # .option("quote", "\"")       # keep text safe
    # .option("escape", "\\")      # prevent quote breakage
    .csv(out_addresses)
